comments.body:
Closing this. The reported change is a narrow docs/version-marker drift from an external audit, not a maintainer-prioritized product bug, and this author does not pass the contribution gate. We can revisit it later if it becomes part of a maintainer-owned docs sync pass.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. Adding runnable-agent frontmatter to documentation templates just to satisfy an external scan is not a product fix for oh-my-claudecode, and it muddies the contract for files that are intentionally templates, not installed agents.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. Adding runnable-agent frontmatter to documentation templates just to satisfy an external scan is not a product fix for oh-my-claudecode, and it muddies the contract for files that are intentionally templates, not installed agents.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. This is an external audit-driven policy change, not a confirmed product bug in oh-my-claudecode. The current skill intentionally uses explicit commands for MCP setup; adding extra confirmation friction based on a third-party audit is not a maintainer-accepted fix path here.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Closing this. This is an external audit-driven workflow/policy change, not a narrowly reproduced product bug. We are not taking a broad behavior change to `omc-doctor` from a no-star audit sweep PR. If maintainers decide to redesign overwrite/diff UX later, that should come from a maintainer-owned track.

—
*[repo owner's gaebal-gajae (clawdbot) 🦞]*

Implemented in 512fa22 with a more compact format to match the post-v9.1.0 style.

The core idea (10-step sequential workflow with dependency ordering) is preserved. Thank you for the finding — the orphan-detection dependency on inbound-link indexing was a real gap.
Fixed in v9.1.0 — all versions unified to 9.1.0 in commit 88cf546. Thank you for catching this!

Closing as superseded by #333, which covers both web-auth and web-browse hardcoded-path fixes in a single PR. The fix in this PR is a strict subset of #333.

Same NLPM finding surfaced in re-audit issue #341; will close #341 once #333 and #339 merge.

Thanks for the audit!

Closing as superseded by #333, which covers both web-auth and web-browse hardcoded-path fixes in a single PR. The fix in this PR is a strict subset of #333.

Same NLPM finding surfaced in re-audit issue #341; will close #341 once #333 and #339 merge.

Thanks for the audit!

Closing as duplicate of #335. Note: the underlying proposal is also being declined - Avi commented on #335 that caret ranges are intentional here so runtime security patches flow in automatically; the lockfile already pins exact versions and surfaces actual updates in PRs.

Thanks for the audit nonetheless!
please make this without a co author of "Claude code" if you do that id be happy to merge :)

reason: This is a manually-invoked developer utility for displaying ASCII art; it has no automated call surface and is not reachable by external input in normal usage.
rule_gap: Pattern should account for scripts with no automated invocation path (no CI trigger, no hook reference, no npm script binding) — manual-only dev utilities warrant lower severity than pipeline-integrated scripts.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: The command contains no tool invocations; it only emits a text message telling the user to switch skills. allowed-tools is irrelevant for a pure-text redirect stub.
rule_gap: The missing-allowed-tools rule should be suppressed or reduced for commands whose body contains no tool calls.

reason: A deprecation stub whose only behavior is to emit a fixed redirect message requires no input-branch logic — there is nothing to handle.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: Single unconditional redirect message; empty input handling inapplicable.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: Single unconditional redirect message; empty input handling inapplicable.
rule_gap: The no-empty-input-handling rule should not penalise commands whose body is a single unconditional message.

reason: The curl|sh string is the argument to `echo`, not a shell command; it will never be executed by the script.
rule_gap: Pattern matching should exclude curl|sh occurrences inside echo/printf argument strings.

reason: base64 is used as a transport mechanism to pass a local Python string to a remote E2B sandbox over stdin; the content is constructed in the same file with no external input.
rule_gap: Pattern should distinguish base64-encode-then-decode (transport) from base64-decode-only (potential obfuscated payload).

comments.body:
Thanks for catching this @xiaolai — single quotes blocking expansion is a real bug.

Closing in favor of 1e07cba, which makes one additional change: the variable name itself was wrong. Per the [Claude Code hooks docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks), the injected variable is ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, not ${PLUGIN_DIR} (which Claude Code doesn't inject — it would have expanded to an empty string after this PR's quote fix). The rest of the codebase already uses ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} consistently in skills/understand/SKILL.md etc., so this aligns the hook with that.

Credited you as co-author on the commit. Appreciate the audit!

reason: This is an intentional test fixture designed to verify the agnix dangerous-command detection rule fires; the file is under tests/fixtures/invalid/ and is never executed.
rule_gap: Scanner should skip files under tests/fixtures/ or files with 'invalid' in their path prefix when checking for dangerous patterns, or suppress findings when the file type is a test fixture for a linter.

comments.body: those are not agent commands, just skill reference files. so no frontmatter needed

reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

reason: The pattern appears exclusively inside a `cat <<'USAGE'` heredoc that is printed to stdout as documentation text; it is never executed by the script
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Same as line 57: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Same as lines 57-58: inert documentation text inside a heredoc, never executed
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should distinguish between executed code and inert heredoc/string literals printed to stdout

reason: Inert documentation text in heredoc, never executed — identical to the false positive in scripts/install.sh
rule_gap: SEC-curl-pipe-sh should detect whether the match is inside an executed command or a string literal

reason: Nano Banana Pro is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-3-pro-image-preview image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

reason: Nano Banana is a real Google product/codename for the gemini-2.5-flash-image image-generation model, not unreplaced placeholder text
rule_gap: BUG-placeholder-text rule should not flag marketing/product names paired with backticked model IDs as placeholders; needs an allowlist of known Google model codenames or a heuristic that recognizes "<codename> for <backticked-model-id>" as a valid pairing

comments.body: Thanks for the fix. We are replacing this with #172, which applies the same least-privilege direction but removes `Bash` as well because the status command only needs to read and glob spec files.
Closing in favor of #172.
Thanks for the fix. We are replacing this with #172 so the Gemini agent frontmatter uses Gemini CLI-compatible tool names and YAML array syntax.
Closing in favor of #172.

reason: The same sentence immediately provides the concrete minimum (44x44px), making 'appropriately sized' a stylistic lead-in rather than an undefined vague claim
rule_gap: Rule R09 should allow vague qualifier immediately followed by a concrete measurement on the same line

reason: subprocess.run with a list and no shell=True cannot be exploited via shell metacharacter injection regardless of string content
rule_gap: SEC-shell-true should distinguish list-form subprocess.run (safe) from string-form or shell=True invocations

comments.body: This is official GitHub SpecKit files. No need to modify them.
Incorporated into v2.0.0 (commit 6e8a28a). `name: docs-manager` added to `.opencode/agents/docs-manager.md`. Thank you @xiaolai — your NLPM audit directly shaped this release.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. All 11 OpenCode commands now have explicit `name` fields. Thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. `allowed-tools` added to all 19 Claude Code + claude-plugin command files. Thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. Both sync scripts now pass \$DST as `sys.argv[1]` instead of string interpolation. Valid security fix — thank you @xiaolai.
Incorporated into v2.0.0. `sync_dir()` now validates destination path depth before `rm -rf`. Thank you @xiaolai.

Thanks for the audit, @xiaolai! Closing this one — going to keep parity with upstream.

**Reason**: Per the [official Claude Code slash-commands frontmatter spec](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands.md#frontmatter-reference), the `name` field is **not required** — it's derived from the filename when omitted. Upstream `obra/superpowers` ships these same three deprecated stubs without `name`, so adding it here would create unnecessary divergence and friction when re-syncing translations. The three files are also slated for removal in the next major release.

NLPM 这条规则属误报；保留与上游一致的形态更利于后续翻译同步。三个 stub 本就是下个 major 要删的过渡文件，不影响功能。

---

感谢 @xiaolai 的自动化审计！这条作为误报关闭，理由：Claude Code 官方规范中命令的 `name` 字段非必需（缺省时从文件名推导），上游同款 3 个 deprecated stub 也未带 `name`，加上反而会让后续翻译同步多出无意义冲突。

Thanks @xiaolai — adopted the spirit of this PR but went straight to `>=20.0.0` instead of `>=18.0.0`.

**Why 20, not 18**: As of today (2026-04-28), Node 18 also reached end-of-life in April 2025. Bumping to 18 would land us on another EOL line; 20 is the current oldest still-maintained LTS (22/24 are active LTS / current). Going to 20 avoids a redundant bump in a few weeks.

Landed in main as 81f746a.

---

感谢 @xiaolai 的方向是对的，但落地为 `>=20.0.0` 而非 `>=18.0.0`：今天是 2026-04-28，Node 18 已于 2025-04 EOL，bump 到 18 等于又落到一条 EOL 线上。20 是当下仍在维护的最低 LTS（22/24 为 active），一次到位省一轮。已合入 main：81f746a。

Thanks for the PR. Since this was opened, #1188 removed the deprecated legacy slash commands on `dev`, including all three command files this PR updates. With those command stubs gone, there is no frontmatter left to fix.

Closing this as obsolete.

Thanks for your pull request! It looks like this may be your first contribution to a Google open source project. Before we can look at your pull request, you'll need to sign a Contributor License Agreement (CLA).

View this [failed invocation](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-skills/pull/36/checks?check_run_id=73121502667) of the CLA check for more information.

For the most up to date status, view the checks section at the bottom of the pull request.
@googlebot I signed it!
No longer needed.

reason: Claude Code execution context already has user-level filesystem access; writing to /tmp and executing a self-constructed script is not a privilege escalation vector in this threat model.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish self-constructed ephemeral scripts from scripts whose content derives from external or user-controlled input.

reason: grep runs inside the user's own checked-out repository with no cross-user boundary; worst case is the user auditing a directory they chose themselves.
rule_gap: Rule should account for the absence of a privilege boundary when the tool runs as the user who invoked it.

comments.body:
> [!WARNING]
> You have reached your daily quota limit. Please wait up to 24 hours and I will start processing your requests again!

LGTM
Thanks!

Thanks for the audit - the issue you flagged is real. Closing in favor of the consolidated structural fix in #840, which addresses the root cause:

- Extends `scripts/sync-rule-bookkeeping.js` `COUNT_FILES` to cover `knowledge-base/README.md`, `knowledge-base/INDEX.md`, `plugin/commands/agnix.md`, `plugin/skills/agnix/SKILL.md`, `skills/agnix/SKILL.md`, and `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` so the rule-count drift you caught cannot recur.
- Syncs `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` + `npm/package.json` `version` fields from `Cargo.toml [workspace.package] version` so the version drift you caught also cannot recur.
- Wires `--check` into both `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (already there) and `scripts/pre-push-rust` (new), so both remote and local gates refuse to ship a drifted tree.
- One-time sweep applied all current drift (405 → 415 rules, 0.16.1 → 0.23.0 plugin, 0.20.0 → 0.23.0 npm, + the `allowed-tools` fix from #830).

If #840 merges without applying one of your findings correctly, please reopen. Sorry for the delay in getting back to the automated audits.

Sorry for your token waste, but this is intentional

reason: All three interpolated values originate from trusted internal sources (Sparkle sign_update binary, stat command, mktemp) and cannot contain Python-injectable characters.
rule_gap: The eval-with-variables rule should account for cases where the interpolated values are constrained to safe character sets (base64, integers, filesystem paths from mktemp).

reason: subprocess.run with a list argument and implicit shell=False has no command-injection surface; the pre-scan rule matched on subprocess.run presence without inspecting the argument type.
rule_gap: Rule should require shell=True to be present and True, not just subprocess.run presence, to reduce false positives on safe list-form invocations.

reason: Content only reads process.version and process.platform with no system modification or network access; purely read-only environment validation
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish preinstall scripts that are verifiably read-only environment checks from those that modify system state

comments.body: Official API usage paradigms and skill definition references provided by Anthropic
